title: Add other vehicle details, multiple witnesses, and video uploader to main report form

requirements.summary: Add an 'Other Vehicle' section to the AccidentReportForm that collects driver contact details, insurance information, and vehicle details — mirroring the structure and field style of the existing vehicle section
acceptanceCriteria: The 'Other Vehicle' section appears in the AccidentReportForm with a clearly labelled heading
Driver contact fields include: full name, phone number, email, and address
Insurance fields include: insurer name, policy number, and claim reference
Vehicle detail fields include: make, model, year, colour, and licence plate — matching the layout and styling of the existing vehicle section
All new fields are persisted to the backend AccidentReport type and returned when a report is fetched
New report detail view renders the other vehicle section with all submitted data

file_operations.description: Add an 'Other Vehicle' section with form fields for driver contact details (name, phone, email, address), insurance information (insurer name, policy number, claim reference), and vehicle details (make, model, year, colour, licence plate, MOT/registration info). Use the same Input/Label/Card components and styling patterns as the existing vehicle section. Add state management for otherVehicle data. Update the createReport mutation to pass otherVehicle data to the backend via the existing otherVehicle parameter.

Add a collapsible 'Other Vehicle' Card section that displays the other vehicle details when report.otherVehicle is present. Render driver contact information, insurance details, and vehicle specifications in a structured layout matching the existing vehicle info display pattern. Use the same typography and spacing as other report sections.

requirements.summary: Add a dynamic multiple-witness section to the AccidentReportForm that allows users to add, edit, and remove individual witness entries with name, contact info, and statement fields
acceptanceCriteria: Users can add multiple witnesses using an 'Add Witness' button
Each witness entry has fields for: full name, phone number, email, and a statement text area
Users can remove individual witness entries
Witness data is stored as an array on the backend AccidentReport type
Submitted witness entries are displayed in the report detail view
At least one witness entry can be added and removed without error

file_operations.description: Add a 'Witnesses' section with dynamic witness entries. Add state management for a witnesses array. Render each witness entry with Input fields for name, phone, email, and address, plus a Textarea for the statement. Add an 'Add Witness' Button that appends a new empty witness object to the array. Add a 'Remove' Button for each witness entry. Update the createReport mutation to pass the witnesses array to the backend via the existing witnessDetails parameter.

Add a collapsible 'Witnesses' Card section that renders all witness entries from report.witnesses array. Display each witness's name, contact information (phone, email, address), and statement in a structured Card or list format. Show a message if no witnesses were recorded.

requirements.summary: Add a video file uploader to the AccidentReportForm that accepts relevant video footage, displays the filename with a remove option, and stores the video as a blob on the backend
acceptanceCriteria: A video upload field is present in the AccidentReportForm with a clearly labelled heading
The uploader accepts MP4, MOV, AVI, and WebM file formats
After a file is selected, its filename is displayed with an option to remove it
Video data is persisted to the backend AccidentReport type
The report detail view indicates when a video has been uploaded and allows viewing or downloading it

file_operations.description: Add a 'Video Evidence' section with a file input accepting video/mp4, video/quicktime, video/x-msvideo, and video/webm. Add state management for a videos array holding ExternalBlob instances. When a file is selected, convert it to ExternalBlob using ExternalBlob.fromBytes() and add it to the videos array. Display the filename for each selected video with a Remove Button. Update the createReport mutation to pass the videos array to the backend via the existing videoFiles parameter. Use the blob-storage component's ExternalBlob class. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add a 'Video Evidence' Card section that checks if report.videos array is present and non-empty. For each video ExternalBlob, display a video player using video.getDirectURL() as the src attribute, or provide a download link. Show a message if no videos were uploaded. Use the blob-storage component to access video URLs. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Move the injury photo uploader to a prominent position on the main new-report page, ensuring it is visible without scrolling or clearly signposted near the top of the form
acceptanceCriteria: The injury photo uploader is rendered on the main report creation page
It appears near the top of the form or in a clearly prominent position
Uploading injury photos from this location triggers AI analysis as before
No duplicate injury uploader exists elsewhere on the page

file_operations.description: Import and render the InjuryAnalysisPanel component near the top of the AccidentReportForm, placing it prominently after the form title/description but before the vehicle and surroundings sections. Pass an empty reportId (0n or null) to indicate this is for a new report creation context, or adjust InjuryAnalysisPanel to handle the new-report scenario. Ensure the component is visible without scrolling on desktop viewports.
Verify that InjuryAnalysisPanel is still rendered in the report detail view for existing reports, passing the valid reportId. Ensure no duplicate injury uploader is present on this page.
